creative_brief: Split frame: left side shows a generic supermarket barcode on shrink-wrapped meat, cold and anonymous. Right side shows a hand-written farm name tag on butcher paper (e.g. 'Bundarra Farm, NSW') with warm, natural light.
primary_text:
Every week you buy groceries that quietly erase the farmer who grew them.

No name. No face. Just a barcode and a long cold chain.

FarmThru exists because that erasure isn't inevitable. We built a grocery store with zero wholesalers. Pork from Bundarra. Wild-caught fish from Collins. Eggs from Farmer Brown.

Collect from Brookvale, Mon to Fri.

Own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first.

See the general CSF risk warning + offer document.
headline: A grocery store that names farmers
description: Farm-direct. No middlemen. Real names behind every product you collect.
